title: Async Concurrency Primitives Flow

classes.name: TeePromise
methods: constructor() — creates native Promise, exposes resolve/reject handles
get status() / set status(s) — setting STATUS_DONE auto-calls doneResolve()
resolve(value) — sets status_ = DONE, calls doneResolve(value)
reject(err) — sets status_ = DONE, calls doneReject(err)
classes.notes: Acts as a resolvable/rejectable promise handle. Used internally by RWLock as p_operation and p_unlock signals.

classes.name: Lock
methods: acquire(callback?) — if not locked: sets _locked=true, resolves immediately; else pushes resolve to _waiting queue. If callback provided, auto-calls release() in finally.
release() — if _waiting non-empty: shifts next waiter and resolves it; else sets _locked=false
classes.notes: Simple FIFO mutex. Optional callback pattern for auto-release.

classes.name: RWLock
methods: rlock() — creates p_read:TeePromise + p_unlock:TeePromise, pushes {TYPE_READ, p_read, p_unlock} via push_(), awaits p_read, returns handle{unlock: ()=>p_unlock.resolve()}
wlock() — same as rlock() but with TYPE_WRITE; awaits p_write exclusively
push_(item) — sets mode if queue was empty, pushes to queue, calls check_queue_()

check_queue_() — if TYPE_READ mode: drains all consecutive readers concurrently (each increments readers_, async awaits p_unlock then decrements and re-checks); if TYPE_WRITE mode and no writer_: grants single writer (sets writer_=true, async awaits p_unlock then clears and re-checks). Calls on_empty_() when queue empty and no holders.
get effective_mode() — returns TYPE_READ if readers_>0, TYPE_WRITE if writer_, else undefined
classes.notes: Queue-based RW lock. Readers run concurrently; writer is exclusive. Mode is determined by first item in queue batch.

TeePromise_fan_out: TeePromise → native Promise: new Promise((resolve, reject) => { doneResolve=resolve; doneReject=reject })
TeePromise: status_ = STATUS_DONE, calls doneResolve(value) / doneReject(err)
Lock_acquire_release: Lock: else → push {resolve} to _waiting queue → suspend caller
Lock (callback path): await callback() in try/finally → release()
Lock.release(): if _waiting.length>0 → shift().resolve() (next waiter unblocks) else _locked=false
RWLock_rlock: check_queue_: mode=TYPE_READ → drain all consecutive readers: readers_++, start async watcher for p_unlock, call p_read.resolve() → unblocks caller
RWLock (async watcher): p_unlock resolves → readers_-- → check_queue_() → on_empty_() if no holders
RWLock_wlock: check_queue_: if writer_=false and no current mode conflict → set writer_=true, start async watcher, call p_write.resolve() → unblocks caller
RWLock (async watcher): p_unlock resolves → writer_=false → check_queue_()
LockService_lock: LockService: if name is Array → build nested lock chain via reduce(), call outermost section()
LockService: if !locks[name] → new RWLock(); locks[name] = rwlock
LockService: if opts.timeout → setTimeout(() => handle.unlock(), timeout)
FSLockService_lock_path: FSLockService: if !locks[path] → new RWLock(), set on_empty_=()=>delete locks[path], locks[path]=rwlock
asyncSafeSetInterval: → record ts_end = Date.now(), runtime = ts_end - ts_start
→ if sleep_time < 0: await onBehindSchedule(-sleep_time) → if returns true: return (cancel)
Note: prevents overlapping async callback invocations; drift detection via onBehindSchedule
raceCase: First resolved promise wins → returns [winnerKey, winnerValue] to Caller

key_relationships: RWLock depends_on TeePromise (uses as p_operation and p_unlock signals)
LockService depends_on RWLock (creates RWLock per lock name)
FSLockService depends_on RWLock (creates RWLock per file path, with on_empty_ cleanup)
Lock is independent (no TeePromise dependency, uses raw Promise)
asyncSafeSetInterval wraps setInterval semantics with async safety
raceCase wraps Promise.race with named-key result
important_notes: TeePromise is the core signal primitive: used as both operation-ready (p_operation.resolve signals lock granted) and unlock signal (p_unlock.resolve signals holder is done).
RWLock batches concurrent readers: all TYPE_READ items at queue front are drained in one check_queue_ pass, readers run truly concurrently.
RWLock writer exclusion: if writer_=true, check_queue_ returns immediately — no new readers or writers start.
Lock._waiting uses raw Promise resolvers (not TeePromise) — simpler FIFO queue.
LockService only ever calls wlock() (write-exclusive), even for reads — per comment 'enforcing write locks exclusively'.
FSLockService supports both rlock() and wlock() and cleans up entries via on_empty_ callback.
asyncSafeSetInterval uses a for(;;) loop with manual sleep(), NOT native setInterval — guarantees no overlap.
raceCase returns a tuple [key, value] not just a value, enabling case-switch dispatch on async race outcomes.
All five utilities exported together from src/putility/src/libs/promise.js.
